extraction.one_line_summary: CARAPAZ won the sprint from a two-up break with HIGUITA after INEOS sent them clear early, HIGUITA took race lead by 16 seconds.
tactical_patterns: INEOS planted CARAPAZ in 28-rider break while UAE defended, converting defense into offense through numerical disadvantage acceptance.
UAE refused to chase HIGUITA-CARAPAZ duo after doing early work, forcing Quick-Step alone to pull with no support from other teams.
AYUSO bridged solo without race radio, unaware gap behind was minimal, revealing communication blackout creates aggressive but wasteful efforts.
QUINTANA chased in group of six after losing race lead, showing defending leaders pulled into energy-draining pursuit of superior climbers ahead.

rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: timed sprint perfectly from two-up, waiting until final 100m to come around HIGUITA after drafting entire finish.
rider_signals.HIGUITA Sergio: positive: took Colombian champion to race lead via breakaway work, though lost stage sprint after leading out from 100m.
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: neutral: bridged aggressively without race radio but burned matches unaware gap behind was closing, showing strength but tactical blindness.
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: negative: consulted team car mid-stage then abandoned chase effort, UAE leadership refused to commit resources beyond initial pace-setting.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: lost race lead and forced into reactive six-rider chase group, unable to influence or close gap to leaders.
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: neutral: positioned for reduced bunch sprint taking third, showed presence in hilly stage despite sprinter profile.
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: neutral: finished one minute down in GC group, maintaining podium position but unable to follow decisive break.
